---
title: |
  Mathematical Foundations of AI & ML<br>Unit 5: Random Variables, Expectation, and Key Distributions
bibliography: ref.bib
author:
  - name: Prof. Dr. Philipp Pelz
    affiliation:
      - FAU Erlangen-Nürnberg
execute:
  eval: false
format:
  revealjs:
    width: 1920
    height: 1080
    template-partials:
      - title-slide.html
    css: custom.css
    theme: custom.scss
    slide-number: c/t
    logo: "eclipse_logo_small.png"
    footer: "© Philipp Pelz - Mathematical Foundations of AI & ML"
---

## 01. Title: Random Variables, Expectation, and Key Distributions
- Unit focus and dependency role.

## 02. Learning outcomes
- Random variable language and expectations.

## 03. Recap from Unit 4
- Conditional probability and Bayes links.

## 04. From events to random variables
- Why mappings matter.

## 05. Discrete random variables
- PMF interpretation.

## 06. Continuous random variables
- PDF interpretation caveats.

## 07. CDF as unifying object
- Read probabilities from F(x).

## 08. Support and domain
- Valid value ranges.

## 09. Functions of random variables
- Transformation intuition.

## 10. Joint random variables
- Pairwise modeling setup.

## 11. Marginalization
- Summing/integrating out nuisance.

## 12. Conditional distributions
- Updated uncertainty views.

## 13. Independence in RV form
- Product factorization.

## 14. Expectation definition
- Mean as weighted average.

## 15. Linearity of expectation
- Core simplification tool.

## 16. Variance definition
- Spread around mean.

## 17. Variance algebra
- Shift/scale rules.

## 18. Covariance and correlation
- Dependence structure.

## 19. Covariance matrix preview
- Bridge to multivariate models.

## 20. Bernoulli distribution
- Binary outcomes.

## 21. Binomial distribution
- Repeated Bernoulli trials.

## 22. Geometric distribution
- Waiting-time setup.

## 23. Poisson distribution
- Count process intuition.

## 24. Uniform distribution
- Baseline continuous model.

## 25. Exponential distribution
- Memoryless waiting times.

## 26. Gaussian distribution
- Mean/variance parameterization.

## 27. Standardization
- z-score and scaling.

## 28. Log-normal and heavy-tail hint
- Non-Gaussian realities.

## 29. Mixture intuition
- Multi-modal data patterns.

## 30. Sampling vs population
- Dataset perspective.

## 31. Sample mean behavior
- Stability with n.

## 32. Law of large numbers (applied)
- Convergence intuition.

## 33. CLT (applied intuition)
- Why normal approximations appear.

## 34. Expected risk framing
- Decision-theoretic lens.

## 35. Loss expectation in ML
- Training objective link.

## 36. Class imbalance and expected cost
- Weighted decisions.

## 37. Rare events in ML
- Tail-risk blind spots.

## 38. Robust summaries
- Mean vs median tradeoffs.

## 39. Calibration preview
- Probabilities as frequencies.

## 40. Practical pitfalls
- Parameter misinterpretation.

## 41. Materials example: defect counts
- Poisson/binomial framing.

## 42. Materials example: noise amplitudes
- Gaussian/log-normal choices.

## 43. Materials example: dwell-time data
- Exponential assumptions.

## 44. Checklist for choosing distributions
- Data + mechanism alignment.

## 45. Formula summary slide
- High-yield exam sheet.

## 46. Exercise 1 scaffold
- Compute moments from PMF.

## 47. Exercise 2 scaffold
- Fit/compare two distributions.

## 48. Exercise 3 scaffold
- Expected-risk decision threshold.

## 49. Unit summary
- Concepts to retain.

## 50. References + transition
- Bridge to statistical inference.

::: {#refs}
:::
